rulings.Case Title: JENNY SAI-YUN SHU CHANG ET AL VS. SARA SHU CHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Enforcing Settlement Agreement Or Alternatively Setting Aside Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 12, 2020 line 1. PLAINTIFF JENNY SHU CHANG, STELLA SHU LOO, and PHILLIP SHU's Motion For Order Enforcing Settlement Agreement Or Alternatively Setting Aside Settlement Agreement Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion to Enforce Settlement or to Set Aside Settlement is DENIED. The parties appear to be in agreement as to the sale of the 30th Avenue property and shall proceed in accordance with that agreement. The provisions of the agreement regarding the 22nd Avenue property are unambiguous in that only "in the event that Defendants decide to sell the 22nd Avenue Property, the parties agree to use the realtor selected to handle the sale of the 30th Avenue Property." There is no evidence that Defendants decided to sell the property. There is no ambiguity in the agreement. The parties may proceed with the Probate Court procedures to determine if the offered buy-out is confirmed. There is no basis for set aside a fully executed unambiguous binding agreement between the parties. The Court will not entertain Defendant's request to enter judgment as it is improperly made in opposition to Plaintiff's motion. Any such request should be made, if necessary and proper, in a separate properly noticed motion. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ZENELIA WHEELER ET AL VS. RICARDO GALDAMEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug 12, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANT RICARDO GALDAMEZ's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Quash Service of Summons and to Vacate the Default. is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Vacate based on CCP 473.5 is GRANTED. Defendant to file an answer no later than August 12, 2020 by 5:00 p.m. Motion to Quash is DENIED. Defendant was served by a registered process server and failed to provide sufficient evidence to rebut the presumption afforded to a registered process server. = (501/CFH)
